etymology.description: 晶 (stars) + 光 (light; brightness) + 彡 (light rays) -> Three Stars mansion -> kneeling person observing stars
etymology.interpretation: The character 參(參) (cān, 'participate; reference; take part in') originally depicted a constellation of stars, symbolizing participation by reflecting the observation of stars, akin to participating in something larger. The imagery of a kneeling person looking at the stars suggests reverence and inclusion in a cosmic order, which becomes an abstraction for being part of an activity or group. 彡 serves both as a representation of light rays and a phonetic component, contributing its sound value.
etymology.simplification: Simplified by reducing the detailed components of 參 to 参, omitting parts such as 光 and focusing mainly on 彡.

examples.chinese: 他想参加比赛(他想參加比賽)。
examples.english: He wants to participate in the competition.

examples.chinese: 请参考书的目录(請參考書的目錄)。
examples.english: Please refer to the book's index.

examples.chinese: 我们委托他参加会议(我們委托他參加會議)。
examples.english: We entrusted him to take part in the meeting.
